steps.title: Identify the verb-object relationship
steps.content: The sentence has a subject “Her innovative poems,” the verb “popularized,” and a direct object “the use of Jamaican Creole in literature.”

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content: A verb and its direct object should not be separated by punctuation. Choice C correctly places the object immediately after the verb. Choices A, B, and D all insert punctuation between them. That matches choice C — C is correct.
